rationale: Determinism postcondition `A ==> det_values_agree_equal(r1, r2)` is logically unprovable because the source function's ensures clause `!ret.0 ==> (ret.1 == Q)` only constrains ret.1 on the error path. When r1.0 == r2.0 == true, r1.1 and r2.1 are entirely free per the spec, so SMT finds counterexamples like r1=(true,false), r2=(true,true) where the implication's hypothesis is satisfied but the conclusion is false. Tried: (1) direct case-split with explicit `if (LHS) { ... }` block re-asserting both forall predicates, (2) `assert(det_values_agree_equal(r1,r2))` unconditionally, (3) let-bound P and Q with case-split on r1.0, (4) empty proof body, (5) best-effort proof covering only the !r1.0 case and r1.0 == r2.0. All produced 'postcondition not satisfied' because the r1.0==true branch admits r1.1 != r2.1. The underlying spec is non-deterministic in ret.1 on the success path; closing this would require modifying the equality predicate (errs_equivalent-style) or strengthening values_agree'
